title: Xtra Chewy Umami Australian Meat Tacos
description: This kid-friendly dish combines the flavors of Australia with the comfort of tacos, featuring fajita seasoning and a high-protein twist.

ingredients.name: ground beef
ingredients.notes: 80/20 lean-to-fat ratio for juiciness and chewiness.

ingredients.name: red bell pepper
ingredients.notes: Any color will do, but red adds a pop of color.

ingredients.name: Australian bush tomato paste
ingredients.notes: Also known as quandong, adds a unique umami flavor.

ingredients.name: kosher salt
ingredients.notes: For added depth without overpowering the dish.

ingredients.name: tortillas
ingredients.notes: Corn tortillas work best for this recipe.

ingredients.name: shredded cheese
ingredients.notes: Choose a blend of Australian cheddar and parmesan for best flavor.

ingredients.name: sour cream
ingredients.notes: Optional, but adds a nice cooling contrast.

ingredients.name: nigiri-shaping tool
ingredients.notes: For creating those perfect taco shapes.

ingredients.name: vegetable oil
ingredients.notes: For cooking the beef and vegetables.

instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the ground beef and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spatula as it cooks.
Once the beef is browned, add the fajita seasoning and Australian bush tomato paste, stirring to combine.
Add the chopped onion and red bell pepper, cooking until they are softened and fragrant.
Stir in the Australian beef broth and bring to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and let the mixture cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
While the mixture is cooking, prepare the tortillas by warming them in a dry skillet or in the microwave.
To assemble the tacos, place a portion of the beef mixture onto a warmed tortilla, followed by a sprinkle of shredded cheese and a dollop of sour cream.
Use the nigiri-shaping tool to shape the beef mixture into neat taco shapes, if desired.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
